title: Claude Code behavioral parity audit — extract specs, then build Rust test corpus
description:
## Goal

Use the extracted Claude Code TypeScript source at `/home/doll/claude-code` (1,902 source files, ~200 subsystem directories — no test suite shipped) as a **behavioral reference** for OpenClaudia. Two phases:

- **Phase 1** (15 child issues): per-subsystem **behavioral spec extraction**. Read the TS source, enumerate observable contracts (inputs → outputs, edge cases, error paths, state transitions). Output: a markdown spec inside the issue body. No Rust yet.
- **Phase 2** (15 child issues, one per Phase 1): **implement Rust tests** matching the Phase 1 spec. Mix of `#[cfg(test)]` unit tests + `tests/` integration tests. Each Phase 2 issue is gated on its Phase 1 counterpart landing first.

## Why this and not "translate the tests"

Claude Code as extracted has **zero** `*.test.ts`/`*.spec.ts` files, no vitest/jest, no `package.json`. The closest things to test infra are production mocking modules (`services/mockRateLimits.ts`, `services/rateLimitMocking.ts`, `/mock-limits` slash command) and an internal `tools/testing/TestingPermissionTool.tsx` probe — none of which is a test suite. The source itself is the spec.

## Subsystem partition (15)

1. Tools — file operations (Read/Write/Edit/NotebookEdit/Glob/Grep)
2. Tools — shell execution (Bash/PowerShell/REPL + sandbox)
3. Tools — agent/task spawning (AgentTool, Task*, SendMessage, AskUserQuestion)
4. Tools — MCP (MCPTool, McpAuth, List/ReadMcpResource + services/mcp)
5. Tools — web (WebFetch, WebSearch)
6. Tools — workflow control (Plan/Worktree, TodoWrite, Skill, Sleep, ScheduleCron, ToolSearch)
7. Permission system (hooks/toolPermission + utils/permissions + components/permissions)
8. Coordinator + tasks subsystem
9. Memdir (background memory agents)
10. Compaction service (compact + AgentSummary + toolUseSummary)
11. LSP service
12. Settings, policy & rate-limit handling (settingsSync, policyLimits, mockRateLimits, cost-tracker)
13. Query / QueryEngine / assistant runtime
14. Plugins & skills
15. Slash commands (high-value: model, compact, resume, rewind, mcp, hooks, permissions, agents, skills, cost, plan, thinkback, teleport)

## Out of scope

- `ink/` (TUI runtime — OpenClaudia uses ratatui; the abstractions don't map)
- `native-ts/` (yoga-layout, color-diff — bundled native bindings)
- `vim/`, `voice/`, `moreright/` (lower-priority surfaces; revisit later)

## Success criteria

- All 15 Phase 1 specs landed in their issue bodies as scannable markdown
- All 15 Phase 2 Rust test modules merged
- OpenClaudia's test surface grows from current (a handful of `#[cfg(test)]` + one `tests/integration_tests.rs`) to subsystem-grade coverage
- Gap log: anything Claude Code does that OpenClaudia doesn't gets a new feature issue, not silently dropped
